entities.name: PIGEONPEA
entities.description: Cajanus cajan (L.) Millsp., the crop species for which pollination control methods are being evaluated during germplasm regeneration

entities.name: GENETIC INTEGRITY
entities.description: The state of maintaining the original genetic makeup of germplasm accessions without contamination

entities.name: GERMPLASM REGENERATION
entities.description: The process of multiplying seed stocks of germplasm accessions to ensure their long-term availability

entities.name: EX SITU CONSERVATION
entities.description: The practice of conserving plant genetic resources outside their natural environment

entities.name: PLANT GENETIC RESOURCES
entities.description: Genetic materials of plant origin that are conserved for future use

entities.name: OUTCROSSING
entities.description: The process of pollen transfer between different accessions, occurring in pigeonpea at rates between 3% and 26%

entities.name: INSECTS
entities.description: The organisms responsible for facilitating outcrossing in pigeonpea

entities.name: POLLINATION CONTROL METHODS
entities.description: Procedures designed to prevent unwanted pollination and maintain the genetic purity of accessions

entities.name: CONTAMINATION OF POLLINATION
entities.description: The unwanted transfer of pollen that compromises the genetic integrity of germplasm
